questions.question: A population of bacteria doubles every 3 hours. If there are currently 500 bacteria, how many will there be after 9 hours?
questions.explanation: After 9 hours, there are 9 ÷ 3 = 3 doubling periods. So the population is 500 × 2³ = 500 × 8 = 4000.

questions.question: A radioactive substance decays according to the formula N(t) = 1000 × (0.5)^(t/5), where t is in years. What is the mass remaining after 10 years?
An investment of $2000 grows at an annual rate of 5%. Which expression represents the value after t years if interest is compounded annually?
questions.explanation: Compound interest formula: A = P(1 + r)^t, where P = 2000 and r = 0.05. So A = 2000(1.05)^t.

questions.question: A cup of coffee cools from 90°C to room temperature (20°C) following an exponential decay model. After 5 minutes, the temperature is 70°C. Approximately how many minutes will it take to reach 30°C? (Round to the nearest whole number.)
If a quantity increases by a factor of 3 every 2 years, by what factor does it increase in 1 year? Express your answer as a single radical or power.
questions.explanation: If N(t) = N₀ × 3^(t/2), then N(1) = N₀ × 3^(1/2) = N₀√3. The growth factor in 1 year is √3.

questions.question: A viral video gets shared exponentially. On day 0, there are 100 views. On day 2, there are 900 views. Assuming exponential growth of the form y = ab^t, what is the base b (growth factor per day)?
questions.explanation: Set up: 100b² = 900, so b² = 9, giving b = 3 (taking the positive root). The number of views triples each day.
